author: Abdul Karim, Haji
title: Chihal Hadis, Hissa Suwam
gloss: A collection of forty traditions of the prophet Muhammad on prayers.
notes: 'Forty Hadith' (chihal hadis) on prayer, Arabic text with Urdu — the classic arba'in devotional-hadith genre, free distribution. Dual date 1327 Hijri.

author: Abi Muhammad Hussain
title: Nur-ul-Ainain
gloss: A pamphlet in which the writer tries to show that to recite Khutba (sermon) twice on the occasion of 'Id is enjoined by the prophet Muhammad.
notes: A fine-point fiqh dispute over reciting the Id khutba twice — the ritual-law micro-controversy genre; author/publisher from Rander (Surat District, Gujarat) — an out-of-province registrant on the Punjab register. Dual date 1327 Hijri.

title: Haft Sura Mutarjam
gloss: Seven chapters of the Quran with Urdu translation, with miscellaneous prayers.
notes: 'Seven Suras' with Urdu translation plus prayers — the popular selective-Quran devotional (the commonly-recited suras) for everyday use.

author: Muhammad Bashir
title: Al-Burhan-ul-ujab-ala-Farziyat-i-Ummul Kitab
gloss: A controversial book in which the writer tries to prove that no prayer can be acceptable until Ummul Kitab, or the first verse of the Kuran, is repeated.
copyright: M. Bashir-ud-din, Delhi; No. 37, dated the 21st March, 1910.
notes: A polemic on the obligatory recitation of al-Fatiha ('Ummul Kitab') in prayer — another fine-point ritual-law dispute (a Hanafi vs Ahl-i-Hadith flashpoint); note the author is a shoe merchant, i.e. a lay polemicist (the democratization of religious disputation via cheap print). Composite pagination 196,8.

title: Sahifa-i-Kamila Mutarjam
gloss: A book containing Arabic prayers composed by Hazrat Zain-ul-Abidin, the fourth Imam according to the Shias, with an Urdu translation of the same by Muhammad Hasan.
publisher: Sayad Saghir Hasan, Proprietor, Yusufi Press
notes: The Sahifa al-Sajjadiyya (Sahifa-i-Kamila) — the great prayer-manual of Imam Zayn al-Abidin, with Urdu translation, 368 pp.; a cornerstone Shia devotional text, again from the Shia-oriented Yusufi Press. The quarter's Shia devotional program is comprehensive (hagiography + theology + polemic + this liturgy).

author: Sher Singh
title: Guide to Parsing for High Schools and Middle Schools
gloss: Guide to Parsing for High Schools and Middle Schools.
publisher: Author, Manager, FitzPatrick School, Kala, Jhelum District
notes: An English-grammar parsing guide (English + Urdu) for schools — the English-language-instruction machinery of the colonial curriculum; the imperial language taught through the vernacular. English-titled. '(Designed for educational purposes.)'.

author: Hari Das
title: The Royal Diary, 1910
publisher: Author, Chief Manager, Lahore Bank, Limited
notes: A dated diary/almanac for 1910 (English + Urdu) issued by a bank's Chief Manager — commercial/office ephemera; the modern business-calendar as print artefact (the Lahore Bank connection shows the emergent indigenous banking sector). English-titled. Composite pagination 384,32.
